title: Chip Industry Technical Paper Roundup: July 21
gist: A roundup of seven newly added technical papers spanning near-memory HBM dequantization for AI inference (SK hynix), processing-in-memory for 3D DRAM (Georgia Tech), software-defined-vehicle hardware abstraction (Stuttgart), 3nm GAA-FET SRAM reliability, and three CMOS-photonic/quantum-photonic integration efforts.

insights.claim: SK hynix published a paper on 'StreamDQ,' a near-memory weight dequantization scheme implemented in custom HBM to scale AI inference acceleration.
insights.speaker: Linda Christensen
insights.so_what: Signals SK hynix pushing compute into custom HBM, a move up the value chain that could differentiate memory makers in AI inference.
insights.quote: StreamDQ: Near-Memory Weight DeQuantization in Custom HBM for Scalable AI Inference Acceleration

insights.claim: Georgia Institute of Technology published 'Open DRAM Model—Part II,' focused on enabling processing-in-memory in 3-D DRAM.
insights.speaker: Linda Christensen
insights.so_what: Processing-in-memory research for 3D DRAM tracks the industry's effort to break the memory wall constraining AI workloads.
insights.quote: Open DRAM Model—Part II: Enabling Processing-in-Memory in 3-D DRAM

insights.claim: The University of Stuttgart published a paper evaluating hardware abstraction layer concepts for software-defined vehicles, assessing their applicability and effectiveness.
insights.speaker: Linda Christensen
insights.so_what: HAL research for software-defined vehicles is a marker of the auto industry's shift toward decoupling software from silicon.
insights.quote: Evaluating Hardware Abstraction Layer Concepts for Software Defined Vehicles

insights.claim: San Jose State University and Sandia National Laboratories published a study of self-heating and radiation hardness in 3nm GAA-FET-based SRAM using different substrate isolation techniques.
insights.speaker: Linda Christensen
insights.so_what: Self-heating and rad-hardness at 3nm GAA-FET SRAM matter for reliability in aerospace/defense and advanced-node yield.
insights.quote: Self-Heating and Radiation Hardness Studies of 3nm GAA-FET-Based SRAM with Different Substrate Isolation Techniques

insights.claim: A consortium including MITRE, University of Colorado Boulder, Sandia National Laboratories, University of Arizona, and MIT published work on monolithic integration of piezo-optomechanical photonics with CMOS electronics.
insights.speaker: Linda Christensen
insights.so_what: Monolithic CMOS-photonic integration is a building block for co-packaged optics and efficient data movement in AI systems.
insights.quote: Monolithic Integration of Piezo-Optomechanical Photonics and CMOS Electronics

insights.claim: A group including Rotonium, National University of Singapore, Inveriant, Politecnico di Milano, and CNIT published the design and benchmarking of a quantum photonic chip.
insights.speaker: Linda Christensen
insights.so_what: Benchmarked quantum photonic chips indicate steady progress moving quantum computing hardware toward reproducible engineering.
insights.quote: Design and Benchmarking of a Quantum Photonic Chip

insights.claim: University of Florida, ficonTEC Service, and Colorado State University published work on AI-driven thermal mapping and management in 3D integrated photonic circuits.
insights.speaker: Linda Christensen
insights.so_what: AI-driven thermal management for 3D photonics addresses a key barrier to dense photonic integration in data centers.
insights.quote: AI-Driven Thermal Mapping and Management in 3D Integrated Photonic Circuits
